items.body: Who was tempted in the wilderness, and by whom?
How did Jesus answer each temptation? (looking for: "with Scripture / 'It is written'")
Can you tell the three temptations in order? What happened right after the last one?
Jesus answered with words from one Old Testament book. Which book was it?
What words or phrases repeat? How many Beatitudes did you count?
Who does Jesus call blessed? Are these the people the world calls blessed?
Which saying is hardest to understand? (collect on question cards)
Which Beatitude can you remember without looking?
What did Jesus do when he saw the crowds, before he began to speak? Who came to him?
Where else in Scripture do we hear "inherit the earth" or "they shall be comforted"?
DRAFT — not yet reviewed against the confessional standard.

Match the Blessing: draw a line from each "Blessed are…" beginning to its shuffled "for they…" ending. Check against the printed passage — the text is the answer key. Bonus (D2): two endings are the same — which two beginnings share it? (Bookends.)
Match just three, with pictures: peacemakers 🕊 / those who mourn 😢 / pure in heart 🤍.
Cut the eight Beatitudes into strips, shuffle, and arrange them in the passage's order from memory before checking.
🔍 Listen for a word Jesus says again and again at the start of his sentences. Every time you hear it, make a tally mark. How many did you count?
Jesus is about to describe the kind of people God blesses. Before we read: what kind of list do you think this will be? Listen for the answer.
items.category: What kind of list is this going to be?

items.body: 🔍 Two groups of people are near Jesus on the mountain. Listen for both — who are they?
As you listen, hunt for one Beatitude that echoes the Old Testament — a psalm, a promise, a prophet.
items.category: Hunt for an Old Testament echo.

items.body: Verses 3–10 speak of "they." Verses 11–12 change. Listen for the change and be ready to point it out if no one else does — but wait first.
items.category: Listen for a change in who is spoken to.

items.body: "Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God." — Matthew 5:9
Retell what happened on the mountain and what kind of people Jesus called blessed — in your own words.
Jesus names two everyday things. What are they, and what happens if salt loses its taste?
Why does a lamp go on a stand and not under a basket? What is the light for, according to verse 16?
🔍 Jesus compares his people to two things you can find at home. Listen for both!
"Even so, let your light shine before men, that they may see your good works and glorify your Father who is in heaven." — Matthew 5:16
